# Email templates

Email templates are pre-written messages that go out with a quote. You write the subject and body once, drop in placeholders for the customer name and quote number, and reuse it on every send.

## When to use them

- You send more than one or two quotes a week.
- You want to make sure every quote goes out with the same professional message.
- You want to a/b your emails (formal vs casual) without typing each from scratch.

## Steps to create one

1. Go to **Templates** in the workspace nav. Find the email templates section.
2. Click **New Email Template**.
3. Set a name (something like "Standard residential quote send").
4. Write the subject line. Include placeholders if useful.
5. Write the body. Use placeholders for anything that should fill in per quote.
6. Mark as default if you want this used on every send.
7. Save.

## Available placeholders

You can drop these into either the subject or the body. They get replaced with the actual values when the email goes out.

- `{customer_name}` - the customer's name from the quote.
- `{quote_number}` - the auto-generated quote number.
- `{job_reference}` - the job reference you set on the quote.
- `{quote_url}` - the unique URL the customer clicks to see the quote.
- `{company_name}` - your company name.
- `{quote_date}` - the date the quote was created.

## Example body

```
Hi {customer_name},

Thanks for the chance to quote your job. Here's the quote for {job_reference}.

You can view, accept, or request changes here:
{quote_url}

The quote is valid for 30 days. Get in touch if you've got any questions.

Cheers,
{company_name}
```

When this goes out to a customer named Jane on quote #1042 for a re-roof, it becomes:

```
Hi Jane,

Thanks for the chance to quote your job. Here's the quote for Re-roof.

You can view, accept, or request changes here:
https://quote-core.com/accept/[unique-token]

The quote is valid for 30 days. Get in touch if you've got any questions.

Cheers,
Acme Roofing
```

## Steps to use one

When you send a quote ([Sending a quote](/docs/customer-facing/sending-a-quote)) you'll see two options:

1. **Generate a URL only** - skip the email, just get the link.
2. **Send via email** - pick a template here. Default template is selected for you. Customer email is filled in if you've got it on the quote. Send.

## Tips

- Keep the body short. The link is what matters.
- Use the same friendly tone you'd use in person.
- Mark your most-used template as default. You'll save a click on every send.
